definition: Standard Financial Conditions Indices treat components as independent and therefore understate effective tightening when coincident stock-bond declines raise realized portfolio volatility, trigger VaR-based deleveraging, and reduce risk appetite. A 60/40 portfolio faces ~15-20% higher volatility when correlation flips from -0.2 to +0.3. This implies the Fed may be underestimating actual financial conditions.
evidence: Standard FCIs treat stock and bond components as independent
60/40 portfolio faces ~15-20% higher vol when correlation moves from -0.2 to +0.3
VaR-based deleveraging triggered by coincident drawdowns not captured by headline FCI
open_questions: How large is the FCI mismeasurement in practice — basis points equivalent?
Does the Fed internally adjust for this, even if the published FCI does not?
Could an adjusted FCI be constructed and validated against macro outcomes?
